name: La Parilla Tomato Arbol Salsa
recipeInstructions:
This salsa is typical of what you would find on tables in northern Mexico, where salsas tend to be hot and very direct with uncomplicated flavors. It's perfect for quesadillas and broiled meats, making it an all-purpose condiment that can enliven almost any dish. Be cautious as the heat level can vary.
1. Pan roast tomatoes until they are blistered, deeply browned, and soft.
2. Pan roast garlic until brown and soft, then peel.
3. Heat the corn oil in a medium-sized skillet until hot but not smoking.
4. Fry the arbol chiles, one or two at a time, until puffed and brown (about 10 seconds). Avoid burning them so they don't become bitter.
5. Remove excess oil from the chiles and add them to a blender.
6. Add 3/4 cup chile cooking oil, tomatoes, garlic, and water to the blender. Blend until smooth.
7. Add the Mexican oregano, salt, and cumin, then blend again.
8. Store tightly covered in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
